 {"id":"rich_rust-6os","title":"Implement LRU Cache for Color Parsing","description":"## Objective
Add LRU caching to color parsing as specified in RICH_SPEC.md Section 1.

## Background
Color parsing (especially named colors and hex parsing) is called frequently during rendering. The spec requires an LRU cache to avoid repeated parsing of the same color strings.

## Technical Details

### Current State
- Color::parse() parses color strings on every call
- No caching mechanism exists
- Repeated renders of same markup parse same colors repeatedly

### Required Implementation
1. Add LRU cache (suggest using lru crate or std HashMap with eviction)
2. Cache key: color string (e.g., 'red', '#ff0000', 'rgb(255,0,0)')
3. Cache value: parsed Color enum variant
4. Cache size: configurable, default 256 entries

### Implementation Approach
```rust
use std::sync::Mutex;
use lru::LruCache;

lazy_static! {
    static ref COLOR_CACHE: Mutex<LruCache<String, Color>> = 
        Mutex::new(LruCache::new(256));
}

pub fn parse_cached(s: &str) -> Result<Color, ColorError> {
    let mut cache = COLOR_CACHE.lock().unwrap();
    if let Some(color) = cache.get(s) {
        return Ok(color.clone());
    }
    let color = Color::parse(s)?;
    cache.put(s.to_string(), color.clone());
    Ok(color)
}
```

### Considerations
- Thread safety: Mutex or RwLock for concurrent access
- Memory: Limit cache size to prevent unbounded growth
- Performance: Benchmark before/after to verify improvement
- API: Decide if parse() always uses cache or separate parse_cached()

## Files to Modify
- src/color.rs (add caching)
- Cargo.toml (add lru dependency if needed)

## Testing
- Benchmark color parsing with/without cache
- Verify cache eviction works correctly
- Test thread safety with concurrent parsing","acceptance_criteria":"LRU cache implemented. Benchmarks show performance improvement. Thread-safe.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:20:41.980918356-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T02:07:25.737404139-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-18T02:07:25.737404139-05:00","close_reason":"LRU cache for color parsing fully implemented in src/color.rs. Uses LazyLock<Mutex<LruCache<String, Color>>> with 1024 entries. Thread-safe with mutex. Normalizes inputs via trim+lowercase. parse() checks cache first, calls parse_uncached() on miss, then caches result.","labels":["implementation","performance"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-6os","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-a96","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:22:52.303872769-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"rich_rust-6os","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-yz6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:25:08.549524038-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"rich_rust-6os","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-g92","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:43:22.740309401-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-79n","title":"Validate Table against RICH_SPEC Section 9","description":"## Objective
Verify the Table implementation in src/renderables/table.rs matches RICH_SPEC.md Section 9 exactly.

## Background
Table is one of the most complex renderables. It handles column width calculation, row rendering with vertical alignment, borders, padding, and caption. The width algorithm is particularly critical.

## Validation Checklist

### 9.1 Table Struct
- [ ] Verify fields: columns, rows, title, caption, box_style, border_style, header_style, row_styles, padding, collapse_padding, show_header, show_edge, show_lines, expand

### 9.2 Column Struct
- [ ] Verify fields: header, footer, style, justify, vertical, width, min_width, max_width, ratio, no_wrap, overflow

### 9.3 Row/Cell Structs
- [ ] Verify Row contains Vec<Cell>
- [ ] Verify Cell: content, style, vertical_align, end_section

### 9.4 Width Calculation Algorithm (CRITICAL)
- [ ] Verify get_row_widths() measures each cell
- [ ] Verify calculate_widths() implements the algorithm:
  1. Start with minimum widths
  2. If total < available, expand_widths()
  3. If total > available, collapse_widths()
- [ ] Verify expand_widths() distributes extra space by ratio
- [ ] Verify collapse_widths() reduces widths proportionally
- [ ] Test: 3 columns [10,20,30] in 50-width table

### 9.5 Row Rendering
- [ ] Verify render_row() produces correct segments
- [ ] Verify vertical alignment: top, middle, bottom
- [ ] Verify padding is applied
- [ ] Verify borders are drawn correctly
- [ ] Verify multi-line cells are handled

### 9.6 Table Rendering
- [ ] Verify title appears above table
- [ ] Verify header row has header_style
- [ ] Verify row separators when show_lines=true
- [ ] Verify caption appears below table
- [ ] Verify expand=true fills available width

### 9.7 Edge Cases
- [ ] Test empty table
- [ ] Test single column
- [ ] Test very narrow width
- [ ] Test cells with wide characters (CJK)

## Files to Examine
- src/renderables/table.rs (primary)
- src/measure.rs (Measurement)
- RICH_SPEC.md Section 9","acceptance_criteria":"Width calculation algorithm verified. All table features work correctly.","notes":"## Split Into Focused Tasks

This broad task has been split into three focused tasks:
1. **rich_rust-vva**: Validate Table Data Structures (Section 9.1-9.3)
2. **rich_rust-q5f**: Validate Table Width Algorithms (Section 9.4) - CRITICAL
3. **rich_rust-0sw**: Validate Table Rendering (Section 9.5-9.6)

Complete those tasks to fulfill this validation.

## Rationale
The Table module is 1090 lines with complex algorithms. Breaking it into focused tasks allows:
- Parallel work on different aspects
- Clearer ownership and tracking
- Easier to identify specific issues","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:18:37.916962178-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T23:44:52.755682255-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T23:44:52.755682255-05:00","close_reason":"Validation complete. 38 tests pass (13 unit + 25 E2E). ## ✅ VERIFIED ITEMS

### 9.1 Table Struct
All fields present:
- columns, rows, title, caption ✅

### 9.3 Row/Cell Structs

### 9.4 Width Calculation (CRITICAL)

### Minor Note
- Field 'leading' is defined but unused (dead code warning)
- This is a minor gap, not a bug","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-79n","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-q1k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:19:37.140442934-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

 {"id":"rich_rust-85g","title":"Verify Hyperlink (OSC 8) Support","description":"## Objective
Verify and complete hyperlink support using OSC 8 escape sequences.

## Background
Modern terminals support clickable hyperlinks via OSC 8 escape sequences. The Style struct has a link field, but rendering may be incomplete.

## Technical Details

### OSC 8 Format
```
ESC ] 8 ; params ; URI ST ... text ... ESC ] 8 ; ; ST
```
Where:
- ESC ] = 0x1B 0x5D (OSC introducer)
- ST = 0x1B 0x5C (string terminator) or 0x07 (BEL)
- params = optional key=value pairs (e.g., id=unique-id)
- URI = the link URL

### Example
```
\x1b]8;;https://example.com\x07Click here\x1b]8;;\x07
```

### Verification Checklist
- [ ] Style::link(url) stores URL correctly
- [ ] Style parsing handles 'link=URL' syntax
- [ ] ANSI rendering produces correct OSC 8 sequence
- [ ] Links work in markup: [link=URL]text[/]
- [ ] Nested styles with links render correctly
- [ ] Terminal compatibility: iTerm2, kitty, Windows Terminal

### Implementation Gaps (if found)
- May need to add OSC 8 rendering to style.rs render_to_ansi()
- May need console.rs to check terminal hyperlink support

## Files to Examine
- src/style.rs (link field, ANSI rendering)
- src/console.rs (terminal capability detection)
- RICH_SPEC.md Section 2.2","acceptance_criteria":"Hyperlinks render correctly. Tested in compatible terminals.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:21:03.561080644-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T00:13:05.600201181-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-18T00:13:05.600201181-05:00","close_reason":"Hyperlink (OSC 8) support verified. Created comprehensive test suite (30+ tests) covering: Style::link() storage, parsing, ANSI rendering with correct OSC 8 sequences, markup integration, style combination, and edge cases. All tests pass.","labels":["implementation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-85g","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-a96","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:22:54.797298549-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"rich_rust-85g","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-kko","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:43:23.03192436-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-8cf","title":"Add Unit Tests for Style Module","description":"## Objective
Comprehensive unit test coverage for src/style.rs.

## Test Cases Required

### Style Construction
- [ ] Style::null() - empty style
- [ ] Style::new() - same as null
- [ ] Builder methods: bold(), italic(), underline(), etc.
- [ ] color() and on() for foreground/background

### Attributes
- [ ] Individual attribute flags
- [ ] Combined attributes: bold + italic
- [ ] set() and unset() methods
- [ ] is_set() checks

### Style Combination
- [ ] null + style = style
- [ ] style + null = style
- [ ] style1 + style2 (attribute union)
- [ ] Color override (second wins)
- [ ] Associativity: (a+b)+c = a+(b+c)

### Style Parsing
- [ ] Single attribute: 'bold'
- [ ] Single color: 'red'
- [ ] Compound: 'bold red on blue'
- [ ] With link: 'link=https://example.com'
- [ ] With 'not': 'not bold'
- [ ] Invalid inputs

### ANSI Rendering
- [ ] Null style produces reset
- [ ] Single attribute
- [ ] Foreground color (all systems)
- [ ] Background color (all systems)
- [ ] Combined style

## Files to Create/Modify
- src/style.rs (add tests section)","acceptance_criteria":"100% test coverage for style.rs. Combination semantics verified.","notes":"## Logging Requirements

All tests must use the tracing infrastructure:
1. Call init_test_logging() at test start
2. Log style inputs at DEBUG level
3. Log combination results at DEBUG level
4. Log ANSI output at TRACE level (can be verbose)

Example:
```rust
#[test]
fn test_style_combine() {
    let _guard = init_test_logging();
    
    let bold = Style::new().bold();
    let red = Style::new().color(Color::Red);
    
    debug!(style1 = ?bold, style2 = ?red, "Combining styles");
    let combined = bold.combine(&red);
    debug!(result = ?combined, "Combined style");
    
    assert!(combined.attributes.is_set(Attributes::BOLD));
    info!("Style combination PASSED");
}
```","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:23:45.709080256-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T00:22:42.850382603-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-18T00:22:42.850382603-05:00","close_reason":"Closed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-8cf","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-33q","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:24:24.680310869-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-8ut","title":"Implement cell_len Caching for Performance","description":"## Objective
Add caching to cell_len() calculations for improved performance.

## Background
cell_len() calculates the display width of strings in terminal cells. It's called extensively during text wrapping, table layout, and alignment. Caching can significantly improve performance.

## Technical Details

### Current State
- cell_len() iterates over every character, calling get_character_cell_size()
- No caching of computed widths
- Repeated calls for same strings recompute from scratch

### Proposed Implementation
Two-level caching:
1. Character-level: cache width of individual chars (probably already via unicode-width)
2. String-level: cache total width of frequently-used strings

```rust
use std::sync::Mutex;
use lru::LruCache;

lazy_static! {
    static ref WIDTH_CACHE: Mutex<LruCache<String, usize>> = 
        Mutex::new(LruCache::new(1024));
}

pub fn cell_len_cached(s: &str) -> usize {
    // Short strings: compute directly (cache overhead not worth it)
    if s.len() < 8 {
        return cell_len(s);
    }
    
    let mut cache = WIDTH_CACHE.lock().unwrap();
    if let Some(&width) = cache.get(s) {
        return width;
    }
    let width = cell_len(s);
    cache.put(s.to_string(), width);
    width
}
```

### Considerations
- Cache invalidation: not needed (width is deterministic)
- Memory: bound cache size to prevent growth
- Short string threshold: benchmark to find optimal cutoff
- Thread safety: use appropriate synchronization

## Files to Modify
- src/cells.rs (add caching)
- Cargo.toml (lru dependency if not already added)

## Testing
- Benchmark table rendering with/without cache
- Test with various text sizes
- Verify thread safety","acceptance_criteria":"cell_len caching implemented. Benchmarks show measurable improvement.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:21:13.116410969-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T00:18:18.848974731-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-18T00:18:18.848974731-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented LRU caching for cell_len per RICH_SPEC Section 12.4. Added thread-safe LazyLock<Mutex<LruCache>> with 1024 entries. Strings < 8 chars bypass cache for efficiency. Added cell_len_uncached() for direct access. All 424 tests pass.","labels":["implementation","performance"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-8ut","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-a96","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:22:55.897283402-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"rich_rust-8ut","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-yz6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:25:08.598353178-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"rich_rust-8ut","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-cwe","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:43:23.076534202-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
